label: Sinai Georgian NF M 71, Undertext (Christian Palestinian Aramaic)

note: Early small CPA. Typical hand of Cyril of Jerusalem scribe

bib.citation: Desreumaux, Alain. "Les manuscrits araméens christo-palestiniens du nouveau fonds du monastère de Sainte-Catherine du Sinaï." Comptes rendus des séances de l'Académie des Inscriptions et Belles-Lettres 154 (2010): 1317-1328

Müller-Kessler, Christa. “Codex Sinaiticus Rescriptus (CSRG/O/P/S): A Collection of Christian Palestinian Aramaic Manuscripts.” Le Muséon 127 (2014): 263-309. https://dx.doi.org/10.2143/MUS.127.3.3062095

Müller-Kessler, Christa, and Michael Sokoloff. The Catechism of Cyril of Jerusalem in the Christian Palestinian Version. A Corpus of Christian Palestinian Aramaic. Vol. 5. Groningen: STYX Publishers, 1999
Aleksidze, Zaza, et al. Catalogue of Georgian manuscripts discovered in 1975 at St. Catherine's Monastery on Mount Sinai. Athens, 2005

Müller-Kessler, Christa. “Neue Palimpsestfragmente zu den Katechesen des Cyrill von Jerusalem im Codex Sinaiticus Rescriptusi (Georg. NF 19, 71) mit einem zweiten Textzeugen (Syr. NF 11) aus dem Fundus des St. Katherinenklosters.” Oriens Christianus 104 (2021): 25-66

Müller-Kessler, Christa. “Piecing together Christian Palestinian Aramaic Texts under Georgian Manuscripts (St Petersburg, NLR, Syr. 16; Sinai, Georg. NF 19, 71; Oslo, Martin Schøyen MS 35, 37; Princeton, Garrett MS 24; Göttingen, Syr. 17, 19, 23, 25).” Digital Kartvelology 1 (2022): 1-25

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.

cataloguer.message: Description and identification of undertexts as part of SPP (2011-2016). Migrated to SMDL in 2025.
